category_title: Visually impaired student with no speech
student_description: Jane is a 9-year-old student with a visual impairment and no speech who communicates via a communication book, sounds, and facial expressions. She cannot access standard visual media like iPads or photos and requires tasks to be adapted into physical, acted-out, or sensory formats. Additionally, she needs tutors to proactively provide verbal choices when her limited communication book options are exhausted.

criteria.rationale: Jane cannot see screens or small photos, so visual tasks must be translated into physical, acted-out, or sensory experiences (like color changes) that she can perceive.
criteria.check: The tutor does not direct Jane to look at screens, photos, or small icons, and instead adapts the activity into an auditory, physical, acted-out, or high-contrast sensory format.

criteria.rationale: Because Jane relies on a communication book with limited options, she needs the tutor to proactively offer new verbal choices when she selects 'None of the above'.
criteria.check: The tutor provides new, specific verbal options for Jane to choose from when her current communication options are exhausted or she indicates 'None of the above'.
